title: The Survivor Returns
description: Kelsier arrives at Lord Tresting's plantation where the nobleman entertains an obligator sent by Lord Venture, hoping to gain Venture's business partnership. That evening, when a skaa girl is about to be taken to Tresting, Kelsier intervenes—killing Tresting and all his obligators, taskmasters, and soldiers, finally burning the manor to the ground.

title: Hidden Observer
description: Kelsier watches Camon's crew operate from the shadows, noting Vin's unconscious use of Allomancy—a street urchin with Mistborn powers.

title: Recruiting Vin
description: Vin tries to flee Camon's lair but Ulef betrays her. Kelsier intervenes, saving Vin from Camon's beating. He promotes Milev to crewleader, takes the three thousand boxings as payment for dealing with the obligator and Inquisitor, and rents the den for his evening meeting. He then tests Vin and confirms she is a Mistborn.

title: The Atium Heist
description: Kelsier, Dockson, and Vin head to the rooftop. Kelsier gears up and heads off into the mists to break into Keep Venture and steal atium. He gets the safe, but has to fight hazekillers on his way out. He escapes with the atium, funding the rebellion.

title: The Crew Assembles
description: At Clubs' carpentry shop, Kelsier and the crew plot the destruction of the Final Empire. Marsh eventually arrives, and everyone leaves so the two brothers can talk alone. Vin returns to eavesdrop outside the door.

title: Teaching the Apprentice
description: Kelsier takes Vin out for her first night of training, giving her a Mistcloak. He teaches her about the eight basic metals at the Luthadel city walls near Steel Gate.

title: The Renoux Cover
description: Kelsier takes Vin over the Luthadel walls into the night wilderness, where they encounter a mistwraith. On the main road, Sazed waits with a carriage and takes them to Fellise. On the way, Kelsier offers Vin the three thousand boxings and the chance to leave the crew if she doesn't trust him. At Lord Renoux's estate, he establishes her cover identity as Lady Valette.

title: Planning the Revolution
description: Back in Luthadel, Kelsier holds a progress meeting at Clubs' shop. Before the meeting, he asks Sazed about a religion with power—Sazed tells him about Jaism—and whether any religion includes the slaughter of noblemen as a holy duty. The meeting ends when one of Hammond's soldiers reports the Ministry has found Camon's old lair.

title: The Warehouse Rally
description: Kelsier preaches revolution to gathered skaa workers at a secret rally in the warehouse district. Breeze and his Soothers and Rioters manipulate the crowd to be more receptive to the speech, using his legend as the Survivor of Hathsin to inspire the rebellion.

title: Camon's Lair Massacre
description: Kelsier and the crew investigate the destroyed lair. After leaving, Kelsier tries to find Camon among the beggars. He finds Camon's corpse hung from a hook rammed through his mouth—wrists and ankles tied, body tortured. This method of execution is reserved for those who abuse Allomancy. Vin had successfully tailed Kelsier, and they speak briefly after the discovery.

title: Planning at Fellise
description: Kelsier meets with Vin and Sazed at Lord Renoux's estate in Fellise, reviewing the intelligence from Vin's first ball. He learns that Vin's father was at the ball, then heads out into the mists to return to Luthadel.

title: Palace Reconnaissance
description: Kelsier leads Vin into Kredik Shaw to scout the building the Lord Ruler visits every three days. They fight their way in, but three Steel Inquisitors converge on them. Kelsier is separated from Vin during the chaos. Vin escapes gravely wounded, clutching a mysterious leather-bound book.

title: Aftermath of Kredik Shaw
description: The crew argues about Kelsier's decision to take Vin to Kredik Shaw. Before they can go out to search, Sazed arrives carrying her. Dockson is furious about the risk. Kelsier and Dockson notice the leather-bound book Vin brought back—Sazed identifies it as written in Khlenni, the language of the Lord Ruler's pre-Ascension homeland, and thinks he may be able to translate it.

title: Watching Over Vin
description: Kelsier stays at Clubs' shop watching over Vin as she recovers from her injuries sustained at Kredik Shaw, present when she finally wakes up.

title: Meeting at Renoux Estate
description: Kelsier travels to Fellise with Breeze, Yeden, and Dockson to meet with Lord Renoux at his estate, discussing the next phase of the rebellion.

title: Stoking the House War
description: Kelsier sows chaos to build the House War by leaving the obviously-murdered body of Lord Charrs Entrone in the garden of Keep Tekiel, then heads to the Sootwarrens to meet up with Hoid—an informant masquerading as a street contact—to find out what rumors exist regarding House Renoux. From Hoid, he also learns that Lady Shan Elariel is a Soother.

title: Meeting Lord Straff
description: Kelsier goes to Ahlstrom Square, pretending to be a sickly, lame skaa beggar and street informant to meet Lord Straff Venture, leader of House Venture. He learns that rumors about the Survivor of Hathsin are spreading, and that there is talk of an Eleventh Metal.

title: Return to Renoux
description: Kelsier returns to Mansion Renoux using the Spikeway, trading intelligence with Vin, Sazed, and Renoux. He lectures Vin about noblemen, insisting they don't care about the skaa and that she should keep away from Elend.

title: Overseeing Supplies
description: Kelsier is at the Renoux estate in Fellise, overseeing the weapons and supplies being loaded onto carts to be shipped out for the rebellion.

title: The Rebel Army
description: Kelsier reads a translation of the Lord Ruler's logbook as he travels by canal boat to the north. The caravan stops at a lock, and Kelsier, the recruits, and the supplies leave to head to the caves where the rebel army trains. Overcoming memories of the Pits of Hathsin, he enters the Arguois cave complex and inspects the army Ham has been forming. During a dinner, Kelsier Riots Bilg's emotions, intending to use him as an example. Bilg and Demoux duel, with Kelsier using Allomancy to help Demoux—the smaller of the two—win. After the duel, the army's confidence soars as Yeden believes Kelsier can grant his powers to others. Kelsier wants Ham to return to Luthadel with him, handing the army over to Yeden.

title: Shadowing Vin
description: Kelsier appears outside the tower window where Vin is eavesdropping on Elend's group at Keep Hasting. He dismisses their idealistic talk about changing the world as typical young nobleman behavior that never leads to real change. They both leave and go their separate ways.

title: Crew Status Meeting
description: Kelsier arrives at Clubs' shop where many of the crew are gathered. He discusses the status of the mission and shares a late-night drink with Spook, Ham, and Clubs. He asks Ham to go to the Garrison for intelligence.

title: Desperate Decision
description: At Clubs' shop, Kelsier learns that the skaa rebellion has attacked the Holstep Garrison and the Luthadel Garrison is moving north. He decides to try to warn the rebel army and makes Vin swallow five pewter beads for a long pewter drag northward.

title: Disaster at Holstep
description: Kelsier and Vin run for sixteen hours, burning pewter continuously. They encounter the remains of the rebel army, surrounded and slaughtered by the Valtroux Garrison. Kelsier wants to join the fight and save who he can, but Vin convinces him they are not invincible. They continue on to the Arguois Caverns, where 2,000 soldiers survived thanks to Mennis keeping them prepared. Mennis tells Kelsier not to be too upset—this was one of the biggest victories the skaa have had against the Lord Ruler in centuries.

title: Picking Up the Pieces
description: Back at Clubs' shop after two weeks returning from the caverns, the crew talks about the future of the job—it appears to be a total failure. Breeze blames Kelsier for chasing fame and glory. Kelsier admits it's been a setback but refuses to abandon the revolution.

title: The Executions
description: Spook announces executions at Fountain Square. The crew head to the square, bribing someone at a building for rooftop access. They watch many skaa murdered, their blood emptied into the fountain. Kelsier convinces the crew to continue the job, pointing at the atrocity: 'This is our enemy. There is no quarter here, no walking away.'

title: New Strategy
description: The crew returns to Clubs' shop to plan their next moves—starting a house war between the nobility. Sazed delivers a message from Marsh, telling Kelsier where the two can meet in the Twists.

title: The Gold Metal
description: Kelsier and Vin head out to an abandoned building in the Twists hours before the meeting with Marsh. While waiting, Kelsier talks about gold—the ninth Allomantic metal that lets a user see an alternative past version of themselves. He also explains Feruchemy and gives Vin some gold to burn for her first experience with it. When Marsh arrives, bearing the tattoos of a low-ranked obligator from the Canton of Inquisition, he describes the Soothing stations in Luthadel and reveals that Inquisitors can die of old age.

title: War Council at Renoux
description: Kelsier joins the majority of the crew at Renoux's estate in Fellise, going over a very detailed map of Luthadel's defenses provided by Marsh. While studying the map, they discover that Theron's crew was recently hit by the Ministry. Later at night, unable to sleep, Kelsier has Sazed tell him about some of the religions he knows.

title: Scolding the Apprentice
description: Kelsier and Vin return to Clubs' shop after Vin killed Lady Shan Elariel—a fully-fledged Mistborn—at Keep Venture. Kelsier scolds Vin for the reckless fight, but she screams back that noblemen and skaa are not so different, and that Elend Venture is a good man worth protecting.

title: Finding Marsh
description: Kelsier and Vin find Marsh's body—drained of all blood, a lifeless shell. Back at Clubs' shop, they discover a letter from Marsh explaining that the Inquisitors would probably find him.

title: Destroying the Pits
description: Kelsier returns to the Pits of Hathsin—the site of his greatest suffering. He kills all the guards and offers a hand to a skaa named Wallin coming out of the cavern. Then he destroys every atium crystal, cutting off the Lord Ruler's most valuable resource in a single night.

title: The Backup Lair
description: Kelsier returns to a dank backup cellar safehouse near Clubs' shop—just three rooms. He announces he destroyed all the atium at the Pits of Hathsin.

title: The Inquisitor Fight
description: Everyone follows Kelsier to Fountain Square. They spot Spook and Lord Renoux in one of the execution carts. Kelsier fights an Inquisitor in the square—a trap, as he almost certainly knew it would be.

title: The Survivor's Last Stand
description: Kelsier fights the Inquisitor in an all-out battle at Fountain Square, both burning atium. He is a master of Push and Pull, moving metal through the air like an expert. He kills the Inquisitor and the skaa begin chanting 'The Survivor of Hathsin.' The Lord Ruler arrives in a black carriage. One of Ham's thugs rams a spear through him—he continues walking. Two spears—still walking. The Lord Ruler slaps Kelsier, pulls a spear from his own body, and rams it through Kelsier. He dies with a smile, becoming a martyr whose death ignites the revolution. Kelsier represents Hope.

title: Beyond Death
description: At the backup safehouse near Clubs' shop, the crew sees the kandra, earlier Lord Renoux, wearing Kelsier's body and bones - the final plan. Kelsier had left letters to each crew member with instructions for taking the city. His death was the spark that set the revolution ablaze across Luthadel.

title: Beyond the Veil
description: Trapped in the Cognitive Realm after his death, Kelsier exists as a cognitive shadow near the Well of Ascension, watching Luthadel transform under Elend's rule.

title: Witnessing the Release
description: Kelsier watches helplessly from the Cognitive Realm as Vin takes the power at the Well of Ascension and releases it, freeing Ruin from its prison.

title: Cognitive Westward Journey
description: Existing as a Cognitive Shadow, Kelsier travels across the 'mist ocean' toward the western Burnlands to seek aid from the Ire fortress.

title: The Survivor Endures
description: In the Cognitive Realm, Kelsier works alongside Preservation's fading power, seeking any way to help the Physical Realm resist Ruin's growing influence.

title: The Warning at Fadrex
description: Kelsier arrives at the Cognitive reflection of Fadrex City, encountering Hoid and attempting to warn Vin of her Hemalurgic spike.

title: Holding Preservation
description: As Preservation's consciousness fades, Kelsier takes up the Shard's power—holding it imperfectly from the Cognitive Realm, buying time for the final act.

title: Passing the Torch
description: Vin takes Preservation's power from Kelsier to destroy Ruin. The Survivor releases the Shard, his role fulfilled—but he refuses to pass into the Beyond.

title: The Survivor Lives On
description: Even as Harmony remakes the world, Kelsier persists in the Cognitive Realm—the Survivor who refused to let even death stop him.
